description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden frame and fabric-covered wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century aviation. The structure features two main wings stacked vertically, supported by a network of struts and wires, giving it a classic biplane appearance. The fuselage is open, exposing the pilot's seat and control mechanisms, with a visible engine mounted at the front. The tail section consists of a boxy horizontal stabilizer and twin vertical rudders, all constructed from the same wood and fabric materials. The landing gear comprises two main wheels and a pair of wooden skids, suitable for rough field landings. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed framework and minimal aerodynamic fairings.
description: A vintage, open-cockpit biplane with a wooden frame, fabric wings, and exposed structural elements, featuring twin vertical rudders and simple landing gear.
description_view_0: This view highlights the open cockpit, front-mounted engine, and the intricate strut and wire framework supporting the dual wings.
description_view_1: From this angle, the twin vertical rudders and the boxy tail structure are prominent, along with the full span of the upper and lower wings.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the length of the fuselage, the open cockpit, and the alignment of the landing gear and skids.
description_view_3: Here, the front of the biplane is visible, showing the propeller, engine, and the arrangement of the main wheels and skids.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a boxy horizontal stabilizer and twin vertical rudders.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with two main wheels and wooden skids; taildragger configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit with a single seat and no enclosure.
